commit.message:
fix(feature-svc): fix CORS and rate limiting middleware order

Critical bugs fixed:
- CORS middleware was running AFTER rate limiting, so 429 responses
  had no Access-Control-Allow-Origin headers. Browsers blocked these
  as network errors, showing 'Unable to connect to Feature Service'
- Rate limiter ran before auth, so user_id JWT claim was unavailable
  and Financial policy fell back to per-IP (all users shared 10 req/60s)
- Production .env had CORS__ALLOWEDORIGINS__0=https://www.aivalid.id
  which OVERRODE appsettings.json, blocking https://aivalid.id (no www)

Middleware order: CORS → Auth → Rate Limiting (was: Rate → CORS → Auth)
Rate limit: GET/HEAD on financial endpoints now use Global policy (100/60s)
Only POST/PUT/DELETE use Financial policy (30/60s per user)
Skip OPTIONS preflight from rate limiting entirely

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: ICC mismatch variants now produce output files

encodeImageWithICCProfile() returned nil when CGColorSpaceCreateWithICCData
rejected malformed/synthetic ICC data, causing 0 mismatch files to be generated.

Changes:
- Add fallback encoding in encodeImageWithICCProfile() — encodes image as-is
  when color space creation fails, ensuring output files are always created
- Rewrite encodeImageWithMismatchedProfile() strategies 0-1 to use named color
  spaces (Display P3, AdobeRGB 1998) for real gamut/gamma mismatch on sRGB content
- Strategies 2-3 (Lab abstract, truncated) use synthetic ICC headers that trigger
  the new fallback path
- Result: 32 mismatch files per run (was 0), total output 152 → 184 files

Tested: native ASAN+UBSAN build, 0 sanitizer errors, all 19 seeds produce
complete variant sets (base + no-ICC + mismatch across PNG/TIFF formats)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[loop invariants] fix loop invariant with function calls in && chain (#3741)

## Description

Fixes #3735

Loop invariants of the form `__ESBMC_loop_invariant(A && f(x) && B)`
(function call in the middle of `&&`) were mis-handled: the generic
`remove_sideeffects` path turned the expression into short-circuit GOTO
and dropped guard `A` from the stored invariant, leading to wrong
verification results.

**Fix:**

1. **goto_sideeffects.cpp** — Special-case `__ESBMC_loop_invariant`:
flatten the top-level `&&`, run `remove_sideeffects` per conjunct,
rebuild the `and` chain. The full expression is preserved in the
LOOP_INVARIANT instruction.
2. **goto_loop_invariant.cpp** — Extract DECL/FUNCTION_CALL instructions
for invariant temporaries into a side-effects block and re-insert it
before each ASSERT/ASSUME so calls are re-evaluated after HAVOC.
3. Loops with no modified variables are no longer skipped; the base-case
ASSERT is still emitted to catch invalid invariants.

**Tests:** `github_3735` (all_cases.c: fun first/middle/last in `&&`),
`github_3735_fail` (expect FAILED), `github_3735_multi_fc` (two FCs in
`&&`).

---------

commit.message:
fix: move useRuntimeConfig() into request interceptor (#62)

## Problem

Calling `useRuntimeConfig()` at the top level of `api/index.ts` caused
the following error on every route navigation:

```
Error: [nuxt] instance unavailable
    at useNuxtApp
    at useRuntimeConfig
```

The module is imported before any Nuxt app context exists (e.g. at
server startup), so the Nuxt instance is not yet available.

## Fix

Moved `useRuntimeConfig()` from module top-level into the axios request
interceptor. The interceptor only runs during actual HTTP requests, at
which point the Nuxt app context is always available.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove crossOrigin and prevent GC of in-flight Image objects

Two root causes identified for PNG assets never rendering:

1. crossOrigin='anonymous' on new Image() fails in VS Code webviews
   because the vscode-resource server may not send CORS headers.
   The retry mechanism (load without crossOrigin after CORS failure)
   could also fail due to browser response caching. Fix: remove
   crossOrigin from ALL image loads — tilesets only need drawImage()
   (works on tainted canvases), and character sheet removeBackground()
   already catches SecurityError and falls back to raw image.

2. Image objects created as local variables could be garbage collected
   before onload fires, especially under memory pressure from 7+
   simultaneous multi-MB loads. Fix: add module-level _pendingImages
   Set that holds strong references until load/error completes.

Also adds diagnostic logging: first-render asset state check in
renderer, 3-second delayed asset readiness check after character
assets message.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
